name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.
detection: Monitor executed processes and command-line arguments.

name: PowerShell
description: Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.
detection: Monitor PowerShell process creation and script block logging.

name: Windows Command Shell
description: Adversaries may abuse the Windows command shell for execution.
detection: Monitor cmd.exe spawned from unusual parents.

name: Unix Shell
description: Adversaries may abuse Unix shell for execution.
detection: Monitor bash/sh/zsh process invocations with suspicious arguments.

name: Process Injection
description: Adversaries may inject code into processes to evade process-based defenses.
detection: Monitor for processes reading/writing to another process's memory.

name: Dynamic-link Library Injection
description: Adversaries may inject DLLs into processes to execute code.
detection: Monitor CreateRemoteThread and LoadLibrary calls.

name: Process Hollowing
description: Adversaries may replace code in a suspended process to evade defenses.
detection: Monitor CreateProcess with CREATE_SUSPENDED flag and subsequent NtWriteVirtualMemory.

name: OS Credential Dumping
description: Adversaries may attempt to dump credentials to obtain account login and credential material.
detection: Monitor LSASS access, /etc/shadow reads, SAM database access.

name: LSASS Memory
description: Adversaries may attempt to access credential material stored in LSASS memory.
detection: Monitor for access to LSASS process memory via OpenProcess.

name: Valid Accounts
description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts.
detection: Monitor for anomalous logon activity and use of accounts.

name: Brute Force
description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts.
detection: Monitor for repeated authentication failures.

name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
description: Adversaries may attempt to exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems.
detection: Monitor web server logs for exploit indicators.

name: Phishing
description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems.
detection: Monitor for suspicious email attachments, links, and sender domains.

name: Spearphishing Attachment
description: Adversaries may send spearphishing emails with malicious attachments.
detection: Monitor email gateways for suspicious attachment types.

name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
description: Adversaries may configure system settings to automatically execute a program during boot or logon.
detection: Monitor for modifications to startup directories and registry run keys.

name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
description: Adversaries may achieve persistence by adding programs to startup folders or registry run keys.
detection: Monitor registry Run/RunOnce keys and startup folder changes.

name: Application Layer Protocol
description: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols to avoid detection.
detection: Monitor network traffic for unusual use of application protocols.

name: Web Protocols
description: Adversaries may communicate using HTTP/HTTPS to avoid detection.
detection: Monitor web traffic for beaconing patterns and unusual HTTP headers.

name: File and Directory Discovery
description: Adversaries may enumerate files and directories to gather information.
detection: Monitor for excessive file enumeration commands (ls, dir, find).

name: Remote Services
description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a service over a network.
detection: Monitor remote service connections and authentication events.

name: Remote Desktop Protocol
description: Adversaries may use Valid Accounts to log into a computer using RDP.
detection: Monitor for RDP connections from unusual sources.

name: Data Encrypted for Impact
description: Adversaries may encrypt data on target systems or on many systems in a network to interrupt availability.
detection: Monitor for large-scale file encryption events and shadow copy deletion.

name: Impair Defenses
description: Adversaries may maliciously modify components of a victim environment to hinder defense.
detection: Monitor for changes to security tools and logging configurations.

name: Disable or Modify Tools
description: Adversaries may modify and/or disable security tools to avoid detection.
detection: Monitor for disabling of antivirus, EDR, or logging tools.

name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
description: Adversaries may exploit software vulnerabilities to elevate privileges.
detection: Monitor for exploit-like activity targeting kernel or privileged processes.

name: Create Account
description: Adversaries may create accounts to maintain access to victim systems.
detection: Monitor for unexpected account creation events.

name: System Information Discovery
description: Adversaries may attempt to get detailed information about the operating system and hardware.
detection: Monitor for commands like uname, systeminfo, hostnamectl.

name: Network Service Discovery
description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts.
detection: Monitor for port scanning activity (nmap, masscan).

name: Obfuscated Files or Information
description: Adversaries may attempt to make an executable or file difficult to detect or analyze.
detection: Monitor for encoded payloads, packed executables, and high-entropy files.

name: Ingress Tool Transfer
description: Adversaries may transfer tools or other files from an external system into a compromised environment.
detection: Monitor for unexpected file downloads via curl, wget, certutil.
